Important Information
Important Information
eShop Manual
Important Information
Please read this manual carefully before using the software.

This manual is a supplement to the Wii U Operations Manual included with your system and provides additional information about your Wii U™ system and features.

Also, before using this software, please read the content of the Health and Safety Information application on the Wii U Menu. It contains important information that will help you enjoy this software.

© 2012 Nintendo.
Trademarks are property of their respective owners. Wii U is a trademark of Nintendo.
Close Manual
Contents
About Nintendo eShop
eShop Manual
About Nintendo eShop

Nintendo eShop is an online store where you can purchase downloadable software and other content for the Wii U console. You can also view detailed information and introductory videos about software titles, as well as download demos.
◆ You will need to add funds to your balance before you can make purchases. 8

<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If a single Nintendo Network ID is linked to both a Wii U console and a system in the Nintendo 3DS family, that ID will manage the balance and account activity of both, and the balance and account activity will be shared.
Software you can purchase
● Wii U Downloadable Software
You can purchase download-only Wii U software and downloadable versions of retail software for the Wii U console.
● Virtual Console
Retro games originally released on older consoles like the NES and Super NES.
A Notice about the Virtual Console's Special Pricing

Those users who already have the Wii Virtual Console version of a title will be able to purchase that same title for the Wii U Virtual Console at a special price. This offer applies whether the title was downloaded to a Wii console and transferred to the Wii U or purchased through the Wii Shop Channel on the Wii Menu. For details, please see the Nintendo home page.

◆ You can restrict the use of the following features through Parental Controls on the Wii U Menu. For more information, please see the Parental Controls section of the Wii U electronic manual. The Wii U electronic manual can be displayed by pressing in the Wii U Menu and then selecting in the HOME Menu.
Name
Description
Wii U Shopping Services
Restricts the purchase of software and use of credit cards.
◆ will be displayed on-screen when data is loading or saving. During saving, the HOME Menu will not be displayed even if you press .
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The images used in this manual are examples. Actual screens may differ.
Close Manual
Contents
Welcome Screen
eShop Manual
Welcome Screen
All controls are performed by touching the Wii U GamePad screen.
1 Sidebar options
Return to the eShop Home screen of Nintendo eShop.
Confirm and configure various information.4
Your current balance is displayed here. Tap to display the Add Funds screen. 8
You can search for software using keywords and filters like software type and genre. 5
Return to the previous screen.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If you are on the eShop Home screen, the Close button will be displayed. Select it to close Nintendo eShop.
2 Keyword Search 5
3Enter Download Code / Charts / News
● Enter Download Code 11
Here you can enter a download code to download software.
● Charts 12
You can view charts about software that is currently on sale, software others are recommending, and videos that others are watching.
● News
Here you can view news from Nintendo eShop.
4 Icons
Software, specials, and videos are listed here.
Close Manual
Contents
My Menu
eShop Manual
My Menu
Close Manual
Contents
Select My Menu in the sidebar to view and verify various information, such as software that you've purchased and your account activity.
● Help
eShop Manual
Display the software's electronic manual.
About Nintendo eShop
View details about the Nintendo eShop service.
Customer Support
View a list of customer-support contacts.
● Add Funds8
Wish List
Software that you have added to your wish list is displayed here.
Select a software icon to proceed to the information screen for that title.6
Select View List to display a list of the software that you've added to your wish list.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> You can have up to 200 items on your wish list at a time.
Rate Titles
Software that you can rate will be displayed here.
Select a software icon to recommend that software to others if you have played it. You will be asked to answer three questions based on how you would rate the game.
Select View List to display a list of the software that you are able to rate.
Ratings will be calculated, and the results will be published in Software Information 6 and Charts 12.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Users can submit one rating per title. You can only submit ratings for titles you have played for at least one hour.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> There are some titles for which ratings cannot be submitted.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> You can view your ratings by selecting Your Ratings.
History
● Your Downloads
Here you can verify or redownload software and downloadable content that you have purchased.
● Account Activity
View records of the software you have purchased and funds added to your balance. Select View Receipt to verify details about your purchases.

<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If a single Nintendo Network ID is linked to both a Wii U console and a system in the Nintendo 3DS family, the account activity of that console and system will be shared. However, please note that you will not be able view any account activity that predates a system in the Nintendo 3DS family's Nintendo Network ID link within Nintendo eShop for Wii U.
Settings
● Notifications
Here you can configure whether or not you would like to receive notifications from Nintendo in Notifications on the Wii U Menu.
● Location Settings
You can enter, update, and delete your address information using this option.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Your address information is used to calculate tax on your software purchases.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The address you've entered will only be used in Nintendo eShop.
● Language
This option allows you to change the language in which you view Nintendo eShop. To apply language changes, you must close and reopen Nintendo eShop after adjusting this setting.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Language changes will only apply to Nintendo eShop.
Search
eShop Manual
Search
There are three different ways to search for software:
1Keyword Search
Enter text, such as the title of the software you are looking for or a particular keyword, to search.
2Search with filters
Search for software using keywords and filters, such as software type, genre, and more.
3Search by software recommended by Nintendo
You can search using the icons in the eShop Home screen for specials, particular software types, etc.
Search Results

Software titles and related videos will display in the order of most to least recent. Tap a software title to view the Software Information screen. Also, by tapping Add to Wish List you can add the software title to your Wish List 4.
1 You can sort by Latest, Rating, or Name.
2 In the Software Information screen, items that you can download will display with the following icons.
Demo
Downloadable Content
Close Manual
Contents
Software Information
eShop Manual
Software Information
Select More Details to view the software-information screen.

You can view details about a software title, purchase it, or add it to your wish list. Also, for certain software titles, you may be able to download a demo version or purchase a pass or downloadable content.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The options that display will differ, depending on the title.
● Proceed to Purchase Screen9
● Add to Wish List
Tap to add the title to your wish list. 4
● Download Demo

Indicates that a free demo is available for download.

◆ There may be cases where the play time of a demo or the number of times it can be started are limited. Note that erasing and redownloading such a demo will not reset these limits.

◆ The time and count limit is shared by all users registered to that console.
● View Downloadable Content10
● Purchase Pass10
About Rate Titles
Under Ratings for This Software, displayed in the software-information screen, the average of the ratings submitted by everyone in Rate Titles 4 is displayed in half-★ units.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Unreleased software titles will not be displayed here.

◆ For more on how calculations are made in Rate Titles, please see How Totals Are Calculated.
14
Close Manual
Contents
Watching Videos
eShop Manual
Watching Videos
Some software titles will have videos available on the relevant software-information screen.
1Pause/resume button
Select to pause the video, and select again to resume playback.
2Playback bar
Displays the playback progress. Slide to advance or rewind the video.
3Full-screen button
Maximizes the video to display across the entire TV screen. Select again to return the video to its normal size.
4Options
The title of the video (as well as related videos) will be displayed. You can also change the video quality or select Software Information to see more about that software title.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Depending on your Internet-connection speed, you may experience stuttering in video or audio playback.
Buttons and Control-Stick Controls
You can also control video playback using the buttons and control sticks on the Wii U GamePad.
Display/hide tool bar
Play/Stop
Fast-forward/Rewind
Advance 15 seconds
Double playback speed
Hold down
Rewind 10 seconds
End playback
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> You cannot save videos to the system memory, USB storage devices, or SD Cards.
Close Manual
Contents
Adding Funds
eShop Manual
Adding Funds
Select Balance on the sidebar to display the Add Funds screen.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Balances are individual to each user's Nintendo Network ID.
You will need at least one of the following to add funds:
Nintendo Prepaid Card
Nintendo Prepaid Code
Credit Card
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The funds you add can only be used in Nintendo eShop. You will not be able to use these funds in the Wii Shop Channel.

<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If a single Nintendo Network ID is linked to both a Wii U console and a system in the Nintendo 3DS family, you will be able to use that ID's balance in Nintendo eShop for Nintendo 3DS as well as in Nintendo eShop for Wii U.
Add Funds with a Nintendo Prepaid Card
Add funds by redeeming a Nintendo Prepaid Card.
Add Funds with a Credit Card

Select this to use a credit card. You can view the receipt in My Menu ⇒ Account Activity.4
You can also store your credit-card information, allowing you to add funds in the future by simply entering a password.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Please note that if you incorrectly enter this password three times, the credit-card information will be deleted.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If you've forgotten your password, please delete the credit-card information and then store it again.
Review Credit-Card Information
Here you can verify and delete credit-card information.

◆Users under the age of 18 cannot use a credit card. If you are under 18 and purchasing something with a credit card, make sure you get permission from a parent or guardian. Follow the on-screen instructions to make a purchase.
Limits on Adding Funds
Here you can view the limits on adding funds.
Close Manual
Contents
Acquiring Content
eShop Manual
Acquiring Content
Close Manual
Contents
Select Proceed to Purchase Screen on the software-information screen 6 or the Search Results screen 5, and then follow the on-screen instructions to make your purchase.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> After purchasing content, it will automatically begin downloading. You can check your download progress in Download Management on the HOME Menu.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Use of purchased software is not limited to the user that purchased it. Any user registered on that same console can use it.
Acquiring Downloadable Content and Passes
eShop Manual
Acquiring Downloadable Content and Passes
On the software-information screen, you can purchase downloadable content and passes for that software title. 9
Purchasing Downloadable Content
You can purchase downloadable content if it is available.
● Search Downloadable Content
The downloadable content available for a software title can be filtered by keyword, genre, and price.
● More Details
Select to display more details about that downloadable content.
● Check box

Tap next to the downloadable content you want to purchase, and it will become . After selecting View Checked Items, select Next to proceed to the purchase screen. Please follow the on-screen instructions to make a purchase.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The types and prices of the downloadable content will vary, depending on the software title.
◆ You can view your purchase history in My Menu ⇒ Account Activity. 4
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Downloadable content cannot be returned, refunded, or exchanged.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Downloadable content, once purchased, can be redownloaded free of charge even if you delete it.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> You will not be able to redownload any content if the Nintendo Network ID used to purchase it is deleted.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Note that if the distribution of a particular piece of content is canceled or ended, you will not be able to redownload it.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Downloadable content you've purchased will be saved in the same location where software is saved.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Use of downloadable content is not limited to the user that purchased it. Anyone using the same console can use it.
Purchasing Passes
You can buy passes for services that use them.

All passes (temporary licenses to use a service or piece of software) have a specific period of time during which they are valid. This period begins at the time of purchase. Should you purchase an additional pass before the current pass expires, the new pass's time will be added to the end of the current one.
● Example 1: Purchase a 30-day pass...
● Example 2: Purchase a 30-day pass before the current pass expires...
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The types and prices of these passes will differ depending on the software title.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If the pass expires, it will not be automatically renewed, even if there are funds in your balance. Please purchase passes as you need them.
◆ You can view purchase history, such as the date passes were purchased (which is also their start date), in My Menu ⇒ Account Activity. 4
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Passes cannot be returned, refunded, or exchanged.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Use of a pass is not limited to the user that purchased it. Anyone using the same console can use it.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If you want to extend a current pass by purchasing another, make sure that you are signed in as the user who made the original purchase.
Close Manual
Contents
◆Please note, some downloadable content cannot be filtered by genre.
Redeeming Download Codes
eShop Manual
Redeeming Download Codes
Select Enter Download Code on the eShop Home screen to redeem download codes and download software.
What Are Download Codes?
A download code is a 16-character code used to download software titles, downloadable content, and passes both paid and free.
Purchasing Download Codes
You can purchase download codes from certain retail stores that carry Nintendo products.
You can also purchase download codes directly from certain online shops.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> The method of purchasing codes will differ depending on the retailer. For details, please visit Nintendo's website.
Close Manual
Contents
Charts
eShop Manual
Charts
Select Charts on the eShop Home screen to see currently available software and charts of the most popular software and videos.
1 Select chart type
The Recent Releases and Recently Popular charts cover the most recent two-week period. The Recent Ratings chart covers the last 12 weeks.
◆ For more on how totals are calculated for ratings, see How Totals Are Calculated. 14
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> If multiple software titles share the same chart position, they will be sorted from newest to oldest.
2 Filter by...
You can also filter software titles by type.
Close Manual
Contents
Q&A
eShop Manual
Q&A
● Purchasing Software

Q

How do I pay for software?

A

Funds will be taken from the balance associated with your Nintendo Network ID. The balance must already have had funds added to it.

Q

Using Coupon Codes

A

If you've received a coupon code (for example, as part of a special offer or promotion), select the Redeem Coupon Code option during the purchase process, and then follow the instructions that appear on the screen.

Q

What can I use to add funds?
A
You can use the following.
Nintendo Prepaid Card
A credit card
You cannot use the following.
Wii Points Card
Wii Points Prepaid Code
Nintendo Points Card
Nintendo Points Prepaid Code
◆For details, please see Purchasing a Nintendo Prepaid Code in the Add Funds screen. 8

Q

Where can I buy a Nintendo Prepaid Card?

A

You can buy them at retail stores that carry Nintendo products.

Q

How do I use Nintendo Prepaid Cards?

A

Scratch the back of the card to reveal the 16-character alphanumeric code, and enter the code to add funds. 8

Q

What credit cards can I use?

A

You can use VISA and MasterCard.

Q

Can I use Wii Points from the Wii Shop Channel to purchase software?

A

No. Wii Points can only be used in the Wii Shop Channel.

Q

Can I make purchases using my balance on the Nintendo eShop for Nintendo 3DS?

A

You can, as long as your Wii U console and system in the Nintendo 3DS family are both linked to the same Nintendo Network ID.

Q

After purchasing a software title, where can I verify an issued code?

A

This can be viewed on your software receipt. You can check the receipt at any time by selecting (from the Nintendo eShop menu bar) My Menu ⇒ Account Activity ⇒ View Receipt. Please see the Issued Codes item.

Q

Can I view the account activity for a system in the Nintendo 3DS family that is linked to the same Nintendo Network ID?

A

Yes, you can. However, please note that you will not be able to view any account activity that predates that system's Nintendo Network ID link on your Wii U system.
● Software Related

Q

Are manuals available for software purchased on Nintendo eShop?

A

They are! While the software is in use, press the HOME Button, and then select Manual in the HOME Menu to view the electronic manual.

Q

Does it cost money to redownload software I've purchased before?

A

No, software can be redownloaded for free, as long as the Nintendo Network ID used to purchase it has not been deleted. However, please note that there may be situations when software will be temporarily or permanently removed from the service. In such cases you will not be able to redownload the software.
● Data Management

Q

How long will it take for purchased software to download?

A

Depending on the size of the data and the Internet environment, downloading may take a long time.
You can view the download progress in Download Management, from the HOME Menu.

Q

Where will purchased software be saved?

A

It will be saved in the system memory or a separate USB storage device (sold separately). You cannot save software to an SD Card. For details, please see Data Management in System Settings on the Wii U Menu.

Q

Can software be copied or moved between the system memory and a USB storage device?

A

Yes. For details, please see System Settings ⇒ Data Management, in the Wii U Menu.
● Customer Support

Q

Where should I go to for customer support?

A

Who you should contact depends on the issue you are experiencing. See My Menu ⇒ Help ⇒ Customer Support for details. 4
Close Manual
Contents
How Totals Are Calculated
eShop Manual
How Totals Are Calculated
User-rating totals in Rate Titles and viewing totals for videos are calculated in the following ways.
● Ratings
An average is calculated from the ratings submitted in Rate Titles and displayed in half-★ units.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font >Ratings for a software title will be shown after the first rating is submitted.
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> In order to calculate a more accurate average, the highest and lowest 5% of the ratings will be left out of the calculation.
● Software Charts
The number of ratings for each title is calculated, and those software titles that have 20 or more ratings will be displayed in order of highest rated to lowest.
● Video Charts
The number of times a video has been viewed in Nintendo eShop will be calculated, and then it will be displayed in the order of highest to lowest number of views.
Timing of Updates
Results are calculated and updated every three hours. (Newer ratings and video views are not immediately reflected in the total.)
<font color="0196c8">◆</font> Depending on the system conditions, this time frame may change and there may be cases where it takes longer for data to be reflected.
Close Manual
Contents
